agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Telemedicine platform enabling patients to book virtual consultations with licensed physicians. The domain enforces healthcare compliance (provider licensing, medical record privacy) and operational integrity (scheduling, cancellation policies, note immutability).","bounded_context":"Appointment Scheduling & Clinical Records","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Appointment","definition":"A scheduled time slot linking exactly one patient to exactly one licensed physician for a virtual consultation. Defined by a start time and end time."},{"term":"Licensed Physician","definition":"A healthcare provider whose medical license number has been verified by the system and is currently valid, making them eligible to accept bookings."},{"term":"Double-Booking","definition":"A scheduling conflict where two or more appointments for the same physician have overlapping time ranges. This is a prohibited state."},{"term":"Cancellation Window","definition":"The period ending 2 hours before an appointment's start time, during which the patient retains self-service cancellation rights."},{"term":"Staff Administrator","definition":"An internal platform role with elevated privileges, including the ability to cancel appointments outside the patient cancellation window."},{"term":"Treating Physician","definition":"The specific physician assigned to an appointment, and therefore granted access to that patient's medical records for that consultation."},{"term":"Consultation Notes","definition":"Clinical documentation created by the treating physician during or after an appointment. Subject to a time-based lock that makes them read-only 48 hours after the appointment ends."},{"term":"Note Lock","definition":"An irreversible transition of consultation notes from editable to read-only state, triggered 48 hours after the associated appointment's end time."},{"term":"License Verification","definition":"The process of confirming a physician's license number is valid. A precondition before the physician can appear in the booking system."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"A patient can only book an appointment with a physician whose license has been verified and is currently valid.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Booking with an unlicensed provider is a healthcare compliance violation. If this invariant is broken, the system is in a legally corrupt state.","testable_assertion":"Every appointment in the system references a physician whose license_verified flag was true at the time of booking."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"A physician cannot have two appointments with overlapping time ranges. For any physician, no two accepted appointments may satisfy (A.start < B.end AND B.start < A.end).","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Double-booking is a physical constraint — a physician cannot conduct two simultaneous consultations. Violating this corrupts schedule integrity.","testable_assertion":"For any physician, the set of their non-cancelled appointments contains no overlapping time intervals."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"A patient may cancel their own appointment only if the current time is at least 2 hours before the appointment start time.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 2-hour threshold is a business policy that could be adjusted (e.g., changed to 4 hours or 1 hour) without corrupting system state.","testable_assertion":"A patient-initiated cancellation request is rejected if now >= (appointment.start_time - 2 hours)."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"A staff administrator may cancel any appointment regardless of how close it is to the start time.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is an operational policy granting elevated privileges. The scope of admin cancellation authority could be modified by policy update.","testable_assertion":"A cancellation request from a staff administrator succeeds for any appointment in a cancellable state, regardless of time remaining."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"A physician's license number must be verified before they can accept any bookings.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Unverified physicians accepting bookings violates healthcare regulatory requirements. This is a compliance gate that protects system integrity.","testable_assertion":"A physician with license_verified = false cannot appear as a selectable provider, and any booking attempt referencing them is rejected."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Patient medical records are visible only to the patient themselves and their treating physician for a given appointment.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Medical record access is governed by healthcare privacy regulations (e.g., HIPAA). Unauthorized access is a data integrity and legal violation.","testable_assertion":"An access request to patient medical records is granted only if the requester is the patient OR a physician with an active/completed appointment with that patient."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"Consultation notes become read-only (locked) 48 hours after the associated appointment's end time. Once locked, notes cannot be edited.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 48-hour window is a policy decision that could be adjusted. However, the concept that notes eventually become immutable is an invariant — the specific timing is policy.","testable_assertion":"Any edit attempt on consultation notes where now >= (appointment.end_time + 48 hours) is rejected. A locked note's content cannot be modified."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A patient is booking an appointment and selects a physician","when":"The physician's license is not verified","then":"The system rejects the booking and informs the patient the physician is unavailable"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A patient is booking an appointment with a verified physician","when":"The requested time slot overlaps with an existing appointment for that physician","then":"The system rejects the booking and indicates the time slot is unavailable"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A patient is booking an appointment with a verified physician","when":"The requested time slot has no conflicts","then":"The appointment is created and confirmed for both patient and physician"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A patient has an appointment starting in more than 2 hours","when":"The patient requests cancellation","then":"The appointment is cancelled and both patient and physician are notified"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A patient has an appointment starting in less than 2 hours","when":"The patient requests cancellation","then":"The cancellation is rejected with a message indicating only staff can cancel at this point"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A staff administrator views an appointment starting in less than 2 hours","when":"The administrator requests cancellation","then":"The appointment is cancelled successfully regardless of time proximity"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"A physician has a completed appointment with a patient","when":"The physician requests access to that patient's medical records","then":"Access is granted and records are displayed"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A physician does NOT have any appointment (active or completed) with a patient","when":"The physician requests access to that patient's medical records","then":"Access is denied"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"An appointment ended less than 48 hours ago","when":"The treating physician edits the consultation notes","then":"The edit is saved successfully"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"An appointment ended 48 or more hours ago","when":"The treating physician attempts to edit the consultation notes","then":"The edit is rejected and the notes remain in their locked read-only state"},{"id":"AC-11","given":"A patient views their own medical records","when":"The patient requests access","then":"Access is granted regardless of appointment status"},{"id":"AC-12","given":"A physician's license has just been verified","when":"The physician's profile is updated","then":"The physician becomes available for booking by patients"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Appointment is cancelled after notes were already written but before the 48-hour lock","expected_behavior":"The 48-hour lock timer should still be based on the original appointment end time (or cancellation time if the appointment never occurred). Notes, if any exist, should still lock on schedule."},{"scenario":"A physician's license expires or is revoked after appointments are already booked","expected_behavior":"Existing future appointments should be flagged for review or cancelled. The physician should be immediately removed from the booking pool. Completed appointment records and notes remain intact."},{"scenario":"Patient attempts to book at exactly the 2-hour cancellation boundary","expected_behavior":"The boundary condition must be precisely defined: 'at least 2 hours before' means strictly > 2 hours, so at exactly 2 hours the patient cannot self-cancel."},{"scenario":"Two patients simultaneously attempt to book the same physician for the same time slot","expected_behavior":"Only one booking succeeds. The system must use optimistic locking or serialized writes to prevent a race condition that would result in double-booking."},{"scenario":"A physician edits consultation notes at exactly the 48-hour mark","expected_behavior":"At exactly 48 hours post-appointment-end, notes are locked. An in-flight edit that was started before but submitted at or after the 48-hour mark must be rejected."},{"scenario":"A staff administrator who is also a physician tries to access records of a patient they are not treating","expected_behavior":"The administrator role does not grant medical record access. Record access is strictly limited to the patient and treating physician, regardless of other roles held."},{"scenario":"Appointment spans midnight or crosses time zones","expected_behavior":"All times should be stored and evaluated in a canonical timezone (e.g., UTC). The 2-hour cancellation window and 48-hour lock are computed against this canonical time."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Incorrect scheduling (double-booking) leads to lost revenue and refund obligations. Compliance violations (unlicensed physicians, unauthorized record access) can result in regulatory fines and legal liability. The 48-hour note lock protects against malpractice disputes by preserving clinical documentation integrity.","operational":"License verification gating prevents onboarding of non-compliant physicians. The dual cancellation policy (patient self-service vs. admin override) balances patient autonomy with operational flexibility. Note locking reduces the administrative burden of auditing clinical documentation.","user":"Patients gain trust through clear cancellation policies and strict privacy controls over their medical records. Physicians benefit from non-overlapping schedules and a defined window to finalize notes. Staff administrators have clear escalation authority for late cancellations.","risk":"Primary risks: (1) Race conditions in booking could violate the double-booking invariant. (2) Incorrect role-based access control could expose medical records. (3) Clock skew or timezone mishandling could incorrectly lock notes or miscalculate cancellation windows. (4) License verification bypass would be a critical compliance failure.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Patients","impact":"Direct beneficiaries of privacy protections, scheduling reliability, and self-service cancellation. Trust depends on correct enforcement of all rules."},{"group":"Physicians","impact":"Protected from double-booking, given a 48-hour window to finalize notes, and granted appropriate record access only for their patients."},{"group":"Staff Administrators","impact":"Empowered with late-cancellation authority but explicitly excluded from medical record access unless also a treating physician."},{"group":"Compliance / Legal Team","impact":"Depends on license verification, record access controls, and note immutability to satisfy healthcare regulations."},{"group":"Platform Operator","impact":"Bears liability for any invariant violations. Correct implementation is essential for regulatory compliance and business continuity."}]},"confidence":{"level":82,"reasoning":"The specification is clear, internally consistent, and covers the major domain rules without contradiction. Confidence is not higher because certain operational details are unspecified (e.g., what happens to existing bookings when a license is revoked, exact boundary semantics for the 2-hour window). These are edge cases rather than fundamental gaps.","high_confidence_areas":["Double-booking prevention is an unambiguous invariant","License verification as a booking precondition","Medical record access scoping to patient and treating physician","Consultation note locking at 48 hours","Cancellation authority split between patient and staff administrator"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether the 2-hour cancellation boundary is inclusive or exclusive (at exactly 2 hours — spec says 'up to 2 hours before')","Behavior when a physician's license is revoked after bookings exist","Whether 'treating physician' access persists indefinitely after the appointment or has a time boundary","Whether consultation notes from cancelled appointments are subject to the same lock rules","Scope of 'medical records' — does this include consultation notes, or are they separate artifacts"],"assumptions":["All times are evaluated in a consistent canonical timezone (UTC)","License verification is a binary state (verified or not) managed externally or by staff","An appointment has both a defined start and end time","'Treating physician' means the physician assigned to a specific appointment","The 48-hour lock is irreversible — no unlock mechanism exists"]},"escalations":[],"rejection_reasons":[]}
agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Telemedicine platform enabling patients to book virtual consultations with licensed physicians. The domain enforces healthcare compliance (provider licensing, medical record privacy) and operational integrity (scheduling, cancellation policies, note immutability).","bounded_context":"Appointment Scheduling & Clinical Records","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Appointment","definition":"A scheduled time slot linking exactly one patient to exactly one licensed

content.thinking:
This is a straightforward domain analysis for a telemedicine platform. The rules are clear and well-defined. Let me analyze and produce the JSON output.

No contradictions, no ambiguity worth escalating, no missing context that blocks analysis. The spec is reasonably complete for the stated rules.

Let me classify invariants carefully:
- Licensed physician requirement: invariant (data integrity)
- No double-booking: invariant (state integrity)
- Cancellation window: policy (could change)
- License verification: invariant (compliance)
- Medical records visibility: invariant (privacy/legal)


- Consultation notes locking: the timeframe is policy, but the locking mechanism itself is invariant

I'm fairly confident in this analysis—the spec covers what it needs to, around 82% confidence overall.
